html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Ten — Part One

The Book of Yehoshua

YEHOSHUA

The Conquest of the Land

Chapters 1 through 8

The Crossing of the Yarden — The Fall of Yerikho — The Covenant at Mount Eval

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QJosha (4Q47) — Cave 4 — Primary DSS witness for Yehoshua

4QJoshb (4Q48) — Cave 4 — Supporting DSS fragments

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon — Yehoshua preserved in full

DSS primary over Masoretic — 4QJosha reflects pre-Masoretic text form

YHWH and The Creator only — no substitute titles

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 8 — THE CROSSING AND THE FIRST CAMPAIGNS

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 1 YHWH Commands Yehoshua — Be Strong and Courageous — The Charge to Cross the Yarden

1 After the death of Moshe the servant of YHWH, YHWH said to Yehoshua the son of Nun, Moshe's assistant,

2 Moshe my servant is dead. Now therefore arise, go over this Yarden, you and all this people, into the land that I am giving to them, to the people of Yisra'EL.

3 Every place that the sole of your foot will tread upon I have given to you, just as I promised to Moshe.

4 From the wilderness and this Lebanon as far as the great river, the river Perat, all the land of the Khittites to the Great Sea toward the going down of the sun shall be your territory.

5 No man shall be able to stand before you all the days of your life. Just as I was with Moshe, so I will be with you. I will not leave you or forsake you.

6 Be strong and courageous, for you shall cause this people to inherit the land that I swore to their fathers to give them.

7 Only be strong and very courageous, being careful to do according to all the Torah that Moshe my servant commanded you. Do not turn from it to the right hand or to the left, that you may have good success wherever you go.

8 This Book of the Torah shall not depart from your mouth, but you shall meditate on it day and night, so that you may be careful to do according to all that is written in it. For then you will make your way prosperous, and then you will have good success.

9 Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for YHWH your Creator is with you wherever you go.

10 And Yehoshua commanded the officers of the people,

11 Pass through the midst of the camp and command the people, Prepare your provisions, for within three days you are to pass over this Yarden to go in to take possession of the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you to possess.

12 And to the Reuvenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Menasheh Yehoshua said,

13 Remember the word that Moshe the servant of YHWH commanded you, saying, YHWH your Creator is providing you a place of rest and will give you this land.

14 Your wives, your little ones, and your livestock shall remain in the land that Moshe gave you beyond the Yarden, but all the men of valor among you shall pass over armed before your brothers and shall help them,

15 Until YHWH gives rest to your brothers as he has to you, and they also take possession of the land that YHWH your Creator is giving them. Then you shall return to the land of your possession and shall possess it, the land that Moshe the servant of YHWH gave you beyond the Yarden toward the sunrise.

16 And they answered Yehoshua, All that you have commanded us we will do, and wherever you send us we will go.

17 Just as we obeyed Moshe in all things, so we will obey you. Only may YHWH your Creator be with you, as he was with Moshe.

18 Whoever rebels against your commandment and disobeys your words, whatever you command him, shall be put to death. Only be strong and courageous.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) and 4QJoshb (4Q48) from Cave 4 are the primary DSS witnesses for Yehoshua. Chapter 1 is partially attested. The DSS text of Yehoshua generally follows the proto-Masoretic tradition in the early chapters. The text is consistent with the Masoretic in preserved portions.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Yehoshua in full as part of the canonical books. Chapter 1 is the covenant commission of Yehoshua — YHWH speaks directly, commanding the crossing of the Yarden and issuing the threefold charge to be strong and courageous.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 1. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Verse 8 — this Book of the Torah shall not depart from your mouth — is one of the foundational Torah-meditation texts in the Hebrew canon, connecting directly to Psalm 1:2.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8 — this Book of the Torah shall not depart from your mouth, but you shall meditate on it day and night. The covenant commission is grounded in Torah. Yehoshua is not given battle tactics in Chapter 1 — he is given a Torah. The successful conquest rests not on military strategy but on Torah faithfulness. Verse 5 — just as I was with Moshe, so I will be with you. YHWH's presence with Yehoshua is explicitly continuous with his presence with Moshe. The transition of leadership does not break the covenant relationship. YHWH is the constant.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 2 The Two Spies and Rakhav — The Scarlet Thread — The Covenant of Deliverance

1 And Yehoshua the son of Nun sent two men secretly from Shittim as spies, saying, Go, view the land, especially Yerikho. And they went and came into the house of a prostitute whose name was Rakhav and lodged there.

2 And it was told to the king of Yerikho, Behold, men of Yisra'EL have come here tonight to search out the land.

3 Then the king of Yerikho sent to Rakhav, saying, Bring out the men who have come to you, who entered your house, for they have come to search out all the land.

4 But the woman had taken the two men and hidden them. And she said, True, the men came to me, but I did not know where they were from.

5 And when the gate was about to be closed at dark, the men went out. I do not know where the men went. Pursue them quickly, for you will overtake them.

6 But she had brought them up to the roof and hid them with the stalks of flax that she had laid in order on the roof.

7 So the men pursued after them on the way to the Yarden as far as the fords. And the gate was shut as soon as the pursuers had gone out.

8 Before the men lay down, she came up to them on the roof

9 And said to the men, I know that YHWH has given you the land, and that the fear of you has fallen upon us, and that all the inhabitants of the land melt away before you.

10 For we have heard how YHWH dried up the water of the Red Sea before you when you came out of Egypt, and what you did to the two kings of the Amorites who were beyond the Yarden, to Sikhon and Og, whom you devoted to destruction.

11 And as soon as we heard it, our hearts melted, and there was no spirit left in any man because of you, for YHWH your Creator, he is Creator in the heavens above and on the earth beneath.

12 Now then, please swear to me by YHWH that, as I have dealt kindly with you, you also will deal kindly with my father's house, and give me a sure sign

13 That you will save alive my father and mother, my brothers and sisters, and all who belong to them, and deliver our lives from death.

14 And the men said to her, Our life for yours even to death. If you do not tell this business of ours, then when YHWH gives us the land we will deal kindly and faithfully with you.

15 Then she let them down by a rope through the window, for her house was built into the city wall, so that she lived in the wall.

16 And she said to them, Go into the hills, or the pursuers will encounter you, and hide there three days until the pursuers have returned. Then afterward you may go your way.

17 The men said to her, We will be guiltless with respect to this oath of yours that you have made us swear.

18 Behold, when we come into the land, you shall tie this scarlet cord in the window through which you let us down, and you shall gather into your house your father and mother, your brothers, and all your father's household.

19 Then if anyone goes out of the doors of your house into the street, his blood shall be on his own head, and we shall be guiltless. But if a hand is laid on anyone who is with you in the house, his blood shall be on our head.

20 But if you tell this business of ours, then we shall be guiltless with respect to your oath that you have made us swear.

21 And she said, According to your words, so be it. Then she sent them away, and they departed. And she tied the scarlet cord in the window.

22 They departed and went into the hills and remained there three days until the pursuers returned, and the pursuers searched all along the way and found nothing.

23 Then the two men returned. They came down from the hills and passed over and came to Yehoshua the son of Nun, and they told him all that had happened to them.

24 And they said to Yehoshua, Truly YHWH has given all the land into our hands. And also, all the inhabitants of the land melt away because of us.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Yehoshua. Chapter 2 — the account of Rakhav and the two spies — is consistent with the Masoretic in preserved DSS portions. The scarlet thread covenant is one of the most narratively significant passages in Yehoshua.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Rakhav's declaration in verse 11 — YHWH your Creator, he is Creator in the heavens above and on the earth beneath — is read as the paradigmatic confession of covenant faith from outside Yisra'EL.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 2. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Rakhav appears in the lineage connecting the covenant people with the nations who joined them.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11 — YHWH your Creator, he is Creator in the heavens above and on the earth beneath. Rakhav confesses the Shema before Yehoshua's people have crossed the Yarden. Her faith is grounded in what she heard — the Exodus, the defeat of Sikhon and Og — with no personal experience of YHWH's acts. Yet she confesses his sovereignty with greater clarity than many within Yisra'EL. The scarlet thread of verse 18 echoes the blood on the doorposts at Pesach — a visible sign of covenant protection in the midst of judgment.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 3 Crossing the Yarden — The Ark Goes First — The Waters Are Cut Off

1 Then Yehoshua rose early in the morning and they set out from Shittim. And they came to the Yarden, he and all the people of Yisra'EL, and lodged there before they passed over.

2 At the end of three days the officers went through the camp

3 And commanded the people, As soon as you see the Ark of the Covenant of YHWH your Creator being carried by the Levitical priests, then you shall set out from your place and follow it.

4 Yet there shall be a distance between you and it, about 2,000 cubits in length. Do not come near it, in order that you may know the way you shall go, for you have not passed this way before.

5 Then Yehoshua said to the people, Consecrate yourselves, for tomorrow YHWH will do wonders among you.

6 And Yehoshua said to the priests, Take up the Ark of the Covenant and pass on before the people. So they took up the Ark of the Covenant and went before the people.

7 YHWH said to Yehoshua, Today I will begin to exalt you in the sight of all Yisra'EL, that they may know that, as I was with Moshe, so I will be with you.

8 And as for you, command the priests who bear the Ark of the Covenant, When you come to the brink of the waters of the Yarden, you shall stand still in the Yarden.

9 And Yehoshua said to the people of Yisra'EL, Come here and hear the words of YHWH your Creator.

10 And Yehoshua said, Here is how you shall know that the living Creator is among you and that he will without fail drive out from before you the Kena'anites, the Khittites, the Khivvites, the Perizzites, the Girgashites, the Amorites, and the Yevusites.

11 Behold, the Ark of the Covenant of the YHWH of all the earth is passing over before you into the Yarden.

12 Now therefore take twelve men from the tribes of Yisra'EL, from each tribe a man.

13 And when the soles of the feet of the priests bearing the Ark of YHWH, the YHWH of all the earth, shall rest in the waters of the Yarden, the waters of the Yarden shall be cut off from flowing, and the waters coming down from above shall stand in one heap.

14 So when the people set out from their tents to pass over the Yarden with the priests bearing the Ark of the Covenant before the people,

15 And as soon as those bearing the Ark had come as far as the Yarden, and the feet of the priests bearing the Ark were dipped in the brink of the water — now the Yarden overflows all its banks throughout the time of harvest —

16 The waters coming down from above stood and rose up in a heap very far away, at Adam, the city that is beside Tzaretan, and those flowing down toward the Sea of the Arabah, the Salt Sea, were completely cut off. And the people passed over opposite Yerikho.

17 Now the priests bearing the Ark of the Covenant of YHWH stood firmly on dry ground in the midst of the Yarden, and all Yisra'EL was passing over on dry ground until all the nation finished passing over the Yarden.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Yehoshua. The Yarden crossing narrative parallels the Exodus sea crossing in structure and language — the waters cut off, the dry ground, the Ark preceding the people as the pillar of cloud preceded them in the wilderness.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. The Ark of the Covenant going first into the Yarden is read as the definitive statement of covenant theology: YHWH goes before his people, and the place where he stands becomes holy ground.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 3. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The parallel structure with the Shemot sea crossing is deliberate — both involve water cut off, dry ground, all Yisra'EL passing through, YHWH's presence leading.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The Yarden crossing is the Exodus reenacted. Verse 16 — the waters stood and rose up in a heap very far away, at Adam. The location Adam is named — the beginning point of human history is the point at which the waters of the Yarden are stopped. The covenant people enter the land at the place called the beginning. YHWH restores what was lost at Adam through the covenant with Avraham fulfilled through Moshe and Yehoshua. The geography is theological.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 4 Twelve Stones From the Yarden — A Memorial Forever — Yehoshua Exalted Before All Yisra'EL

1 When all the nation had finished passing over the Yarden, YHWH said to Yehoshua,

2 Take twelve men from the people, from each tribe a man,

3 And command them, saying, Take twelve stones from here out of the midst of the Yarden, from the very place where the priests' feet stood firmly, and bring them over with you and lay them down in the place where you lodge tonight.

4 Then Yehoshua called the twelve men from the people of Yisra'EL, whom he had appointed, a man from each tribe.

5 And Yehoshua said to them, Pass on before the Ark of YHWH your Creator into the midst of the Yarden, and take up each of you a stone upon his shoulder, according to the number of the tribes of the people of Yisra'EL,

6 That this may be a sign among you. When your children ask in time to come, What do those stones mean to you?

7 Then you shall tell them that the waters of the Yarden were cut off before the Ark of the Covenant of YHWH. When it passed over the Yarden, the waters of the Yarden were cut off. So these stones shall be to the people of Yisra'EL a memorial forever.

8 And the people of Yisra'EL did just as Yehoshua commanded and took up twelve stones out of the midst of the Yarden, according to the number of the tribes of the people of Yisra'EL, just as YHWH told Yehoshua. And they carried them over with them to the place where they lodged and laid them down there.

9 And Yehoshua set up twelve stones in the midst of the Yarden, in the place where the feet of the priests bearing the Ark of the Covenant had stood. And they are there to this day.

10 For the priests bearing the Ark stood in the midst of the Yarden until everything was finished that YHWH commanded Yehoshua to tell the people, according to all that Moshe had commanded Yehoshua. The people passed over in haste.

11 And when all the people had finished passing over, the Ark of YHWH and the priests passed over before the people.

12 The sons of Reuven and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Menasheh passed over armed before the people of Yisra'EL, as Moshe had told them.

13 About 40,000 ready for war passed over before YHWH for battle, to the plains of Yerikho.

14 On that day YHWH exalted Yehoshua in the sight of all Yisra'EL, and they stood in awe of him just as they had stood in awe of Moshe, all the days of his life.

15 And YHWH said to Yehoshua,

16 Command the priests bearing the Ark of the Testimony to come up out of the Yarden.

17 So Yehoshua commanded the priests, Come up out of the Yarden.

18 And when the priests bearing the Ark of the Covenant of YHWH came up from the midst of the Yarden, and the soles of the priests' feet were lifted up on dry ground, the waters of the Yarden returned to their place and overflowed all its banks, as before.

19 The people came up out of the Yarden on the tenth day of the first month, and they encamped at Gilgal on the east border of Yerikho.

20 And those twelve stones, which they took out of the Yarden, Yehoshua set up at Gilgal.

21 And he said to the people of Yisra'EL, When your children ask their fathers in times to come, What do these stones mean?

22 Then you shall let your children know, Yisra'EL passed over this Yarden on dry ground.

23 For YHWH your Creator dried up the waters of the Yarden for you until you passed over, as YHWH your Creator did to the Red Sea, which he dried up for us until we passed over,

24 So that all the peoples of the earth may know that the hand of YHWH is mighty, that you may fear YHWH your Creator forever.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 4. The twelve memorial stones — one per tribe — are the covenant monument of the Yarden crossing. The text is consistent with the Masoretic in preserved portions.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. The covenant command to answer children's questions — verses 6-7, 21-22 — echoes the Pesach structure of Shemot 12-13: when your children ask, you shall tell them what YHWH did.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 4. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Verse 19 — the tenth day of the first month — is the same day as the selection of the Pesach lamb in Shemot 12:3, confirming the Exodus typology of the Yarden crossing.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6-7 — that this may be a sign among you. When your children ask in time to come, What do those stones mean? Then you shall tell them. The twelve stones are a pedagogical monument — stones that generate questions and thereby transmit covenant memory to the next generation. The covenant is preserved not through monuments alone but through the conversation they provoke. Verse 24 — so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the hand of YHWH is mighty.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 5 Circumcision at Gilgal — The Pesach in the Land — The Manna Ceases — The Commander of YHWH's Army

1 As soon as all the kings of the Amorites who were beyond the Yarden to the west, and all the kings of the Kena'anites who were by the sea, heard that YHWH had dried up the waters of the Yarden for the people of Yisra'EL until they had crossed over, their hearts melted and there was no longer any spirit in them because of the people of Yisra'EL.

2 At that time YHWH said to Yehoshua, Make flint knives and circumcise the sons of Yisra'EL again the second time.

3 So Yehoshua made flint knives and circumcised the sons of Yisra'EL at Giv'at Ha'aralot.

4 And this is the reason why Yehoshua circumcised them: all the males of the people who came out of Egypt, all the men of war, had died in the wilderness on the way after they had come out of Egypt.

5 Though all the people who came out had been circumcised, yet all the people who were born on the way in the wilderness after they had come out of Egypt had not been circumcised.

6 For the people of Yisra'EL walked forty years in the wilderness, until all the nation, the men of war who came out of Egypt, perished, because they did not obey the voice of YHWH. YHWH swore to them that he would not let them see the land that YHWH had sworn to their fathers to give to us, a land flowing with milk and honey.

7 So it was their children, whom he raised up in their place, that Yehoshua circumcised. For they were uncircumcised, because they had not been circumcised on the way.

8 When the circumcising of the whole nation was finished, they remained in their places in the camp until they were healed.

9 And YHWH said to Yehoshua, Today I have rolled away the reproach of Egypt from you. And so the name of that place is called Gilgal to this day.

10 While the people of Yisra'EL were encamped at Gilgal, they kept the Pesach on the fourteenth day of the month in the evening on the plains of Yerikho.

11 And the day after the Pesach, on that very day, they ate of the produce of the land, unleavened cakes and parched grain.

12 And the manna ceased the day after they ate of the produce of the land. And there was no longer manna for the people of Yisra'EL, but they ate of the fruit of the land of Kena'an that year.

13 When Yehoshua was by Yerikho, he lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, a man was standing before him with his drawn sword in his hand. And Yehoshua went to him and said to him, Are you for us, or for our adversaries?

14 And he said, No, but I am the commander of the army of YHWH. Now I have come. And Yehoshua fell on his face to the earth and worshiped and said to him, What does my Sovereign say to his servant?

15 And the commander of the army of YHWH said to Yehoshua, Take off your sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy. And Yehoshua did so.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 5. The circumcision at Gilgal restores the covenant sign to the generation born in the wilderness — the same covenant sign of Bereshit 17. The text is consistent with the Masoretic in preserved portions.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. The cessation of the manna in verse 12 — the day after they ate of the produce of the land — is read as the definitive marker of the transition from wilderness provision to covenant land provision.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 5. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Verse 15 — take off your sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy — directly echoes Shemot 3:5, YHWH's words to Moshe at the burning bush.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 13-15 — the commander of the army of YHWH. The figure who appears to Yehoshua with a drawn sword is neither human nor merely angelic messenger — he is the commander of YHWH's heavenly army. His response to Yehoshua's question — are you for us or for our adversaries? — is No. YHWH's army does not serve human military agendas. Yehoshua falls and worships. The ground is declared holy. YHWH's army does not fight for Yisra'EL — Yisra'EL fights alongside YHWH's army.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 6 The Fall of Yerikho — Seven Days — Seven Priests — Seven Trumpets — The Walls Fall

1 Now Yerikho was shut up inside and outside because of the people of Yisra'EL. None went out, and none came in.

2 And YHWH said to Yehoshua, See, I have given Yerikho into your hand, with its king and mighty men of valor.

3 You shall march around the city, all the men of war going around the city once. Thus shall you do for six days.

4 Seven priests shall bear seven trumpets of rams' horns before the Ark. On the seventh day you shall march around the city seven times, and the priests shall blow the trumpets.

5 And when they make a long blast with the ram's horn, when you hear the sound of the trumpet, then all the people shall shout with a great shout, and the wall of the city will fall down flat, and the people shall go up, everyone straight before him.

6 So Yehoshua the son of Nun called the priests and said to them, Take up the Ark of the Covenant and let seven priests bear seven trumpets of rams' horns before the Ark of YHWH.

7 And he said to the people, Go forward. March around the city and let the armed men pass on before the Ark of YHWH.

8 And just as Yehoshua had commanded the people, the seven priests bearing the seven trumpets of rams' horns before YHWH went forward, blowing the trumpets, with the Ark of the Covenant of YHWH following them.

9 The armed men were walking before the priests who were blowing the trumpets, and the rear guard was walking after the Ark, while the trumpets blew continually.

10 But Yehoshua commanded the people, You shall not shout or make your voice heard, neither shall any word go out of your mouth, until the day I tell you to shout. Then you shall shout.

11 So he caused the Ark of YHWH to circle the city, going about it once. And they came into the camp and spent the night in the camp.

12 Then Yehoshua rose early in the morning, and the priests took up the Ark of YHWH.

13 And the seven priests bearing the seven trumpets of rams' horns before the Ark of YHWH walked on, and they blew the trumpets continually. And the armed men were walking before them, and the rear guard was walking after the Ark of YHWH, while the trumpets blew continually.

14 And the second day they marched around the city once, and returned into the camp. So they did for six days.

15 On the seventh day they rose early, at the dawn of day, and marched around the city in the same manner seven times. It was only on that day that they marched around the city seven times.

16 And at the seventh time, when the priests had blown the trumpets, Yehoshua said to the people, Shout, for YHWH has given you the city.

17 And the city and all that is within it shall be devoted to YHWH for destruction. Only Rakhav the prostitute and all who are with her in her house shall live, because she hid the messengers whom we sent.

18 But you, keep yourselves from the things devoted to destruction, lest when you have devoted them you take any of the devoted things and make the camp of Yisra'EL a thing for destruction and bring trouble upon it.

19 But all silver and gold, and every vessel of bronze and iron, are holy to YHWH. They shall go into the treasury of YHWH.

20 So the people shouted, and the trumpets were blown. As soon as the people heard the sound of the trumpet, the people shouted a great shout, and the wall fell down flat, so that the people went up into the city, every man straight before him, and they captured the city.

21 Then they devoted all in the city to destruction, both men and women, young and old, oxen, sheep, and donkeys, with the edge of the sword.

22 But to the two men who had spied out the land, Yehoshua said, Go into the prostitute's house and bring out from there the woman and all who belong to her, as you swore to her.

23 So the young men who had been spies went in and brought out Rakhav and her father and mother and brothers and all who belonged to her. And they brought all her relatives and put them outside the camp of Yisra'EL.

24 And they burned the city with fire, and everything in it. Only the silver and gold, and the vessels of bronze and of iron, they put into the treasury of the house of YHWH.

25 But Rakhav the prostitute and her father's household and all who belonged to her, Yehoshua saved alive. And she has lived in the midst of Yisra'EL to this day, because she hid the messengers whom Yehoshua sent to spy out Yerikho.

26 Yehoshua laid an oath on them at that time, saying, Cursed before YHWH be the man who rises up and rebuilds this city, Yerikho. At the cost of his firstborn shall he lay its foundation, and at the cost of his youngest son shall he set up its gates.

27 So YHWH was with Yehoshua, and his fame was in all the land.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 6. The fall of Yerikho is one of the most structurally significant passages in the Nevi'im — the sevenfold march, seven priests, seven trumpets, seven days, the seventh circuit on the seventh day. The text is consistent with the Masoretic in preserved portions.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. The battle of Yerikho is read as the covenant conquest archetype: YHWH fights, Yisra'EL obeys, the victory belongs to YHWH alone. The people do not attack the walls — they march, they blow, they shout. The walls fall by YHWH's power.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 6. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The sevenfold structure — seven days, seven priests, seven trumpets, seven circuits — is the covenant number of completeness applied to warfare, connecting directly to the War Scroll's theology of YHWH's battle.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The battle of Yerikho has no military strategy. The instructions are liturgical: march, blow the trumpets, shout. YHWH does not give Yehoshua siege tactics — he gives a covenant ceremony. The War Scroll's seven-advance structure reflects the same theology. Verse 17 — the city and all that is within it shall be devoted to YHWH for destruction. The herem consecrates the entire city to YHWH as the firstfruits of the conquest. Rakhav is the only Kena'anite exempted because she sheltered YHWH's messengers in covenant faith.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 7 The Sin of Akhan — The Defeat at Ai — YHWH's Anger Against Yisra'EL

1 But the people of Yisra'EL broke faith in regard to the devoted things, for Akhan the son of Karmi, son of Zavdi, son of Zerakhi, of the tribe of Yehudah, took some of the devoted things. And the anger of YHWH burned against the people of Yisra'EL.

2 Yehoshua sent men from Yerikho to Ai, which is near Beit-Aven, east of Beit-El, and said to them, Go up and spy out the land. And the men went up and spied out Ai.

3 And they returned to Yehoshua and said to him, Do not have all the people go up, but let about two or three thousand men go up and attack Ai. Do not make the whole people toil up there, for they are few.

4 So about three thousand men went up there from the people. And they fled before the men of Ai,

5 And the men of Ai killed about thirty-six of their men and chased them before the gate as far as Shevarim and struck them at the descent. And the hearts of the people melted and became as water.

6 Then Yehoshua tore his clothes and fell to the earth on his face before the Ark of YHWH until the evening, he and the elders of Yisra'EL. And they put dust on their heads.

7 And Yehoshua said, Alas, O YHWH Creator, why have you brought this people over the Yarden at all, to give us into the hands of the Amorites, to destroy us? Would that we had been content to dwell beyond the Yarden.

8 O YHWH, what can I say, when Yisra'EL has turned their backs before their enemies?

9 For the Kena'anites and all the inhabitants of the land will hear of it and will surround us and cut off our name from the earth. And what will you do for your great name?

10 YHWH said to Yehoshua, Get up. Why have you fallen on your face?

11 Yisra'EL has sinned — they have transgressed my covenant that I commanded them — they have taken some of the devoted things — they have stolen and lied and put them among their own belongings.

12 Therefore the people of Yisra'EL cannot stand before their enemies. They turn their backs before their enemies, because they have become devoted for destruction. I will be with you no more, unless you destroy the devoted things from among you.

13 Get up. Consecrate the people and say, Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow — for thus says YHWH, Creator of Yisra'EL: There are devoted things in your midst, O Yisra'EL. You cannot stand before your enemies until you take away the devoted things from among you.

14 In the morning therefore you shall be brought near by your tribes. And the tribe that YHWH takes by lot shall come near by clans. And the clan that YHWH takes shall come near by households. And the household that YHWH takes shall come near man by man.

15 And he who is taken with the devoted things shall be burned with fire, he and all that he has, because he has transgressed the covenant of YHWH, and because he has done an outrageous thing in Yisra'EL.

16 So Yehoshua rose early in the morning and brought Yisra'EL near tribe by tribe, and the tribe of Yehudah was taken.

17 And he brought near the clans of Yehudah, and the clan of the Zerakhites was taken. And he brought near the clan of the Zerakhites man by man, and Zavdi was taken.

18 And he brought near his household man by man, and Akhan the son of Karmi, son of Zavdi, son of Zerakhi, of the tribe of Yehudah, was taken.

19 Then Yehoshua said to Akhan, My son, give glory to YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL and give praise to him. And tell me now what you have done — do not hide it from me.

20 And Akhan answered Yehoshua, Truly I have sinned against YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL, and this is what I did:

21 When I saw among the spoil a beautiful cloak from Shinar, and 200 shekels of silver, and a bar of gold weighing 50 shekels, then I coveted them and took them. And see, they are hidden in the earth inside my tent, with the silver underneath.

22 So Yehoshua sent messengers, and they ran to the tent. And behold, it was hidden in his tent with the silver underneath.

23 And they took them out of the tent and brought them to Yehoshua and to all the people of Yisra'EL. And they laid them down before YHWH.

24 And Yehoshua and all Yisra'EL with him took Akhan the son of Zerakhi, and the silver and the cloak and the bar of gold, and his sons and daughters and his oxen and donkeys and sheep and his tent and all that he had. And they brought them up to the Valley of Akhor.

25 And Yehoshua said, Why did you bring trouble on us? YHWH brings trouble on you today. And all Yisra'EL stoned him with stones. They burned them with fire and stoned them with stones.

26 And they raised over him a great heap of stones that remains to this day. Then YHWH turned from his burning anger. Therefore, to this day the name of that place is called the Valley of Akhor.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 7. The defeat at Ai because of Akhan's sin is the covenant counter-narrative to the victory at Yerikho — the same people, the same army, but without YHWH's presence because of the hidden violation of the herem. The text is consistent with the Masoretic in preserved portions.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full. Akhan's confession — truly I have sinned against YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL — is read as the covenant model of full acknowledgment: he names the sin, describes it specifically, and leads to the recovery of the stolen items.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 7. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The name Akhor — trouble — is the covenant wordplay of verse 25: why did you bring trouble on us? The valley is named for the covenant consequence of hidden sin.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11 — Yisra'EL has sinned — they have transgressed my covenant. One man's sin is declared as the sin of the entire community — the covenant people are a body, not merely a collection of individuals. The hidden sin of Akhan defiles the entire camp. The defeat at Ai is not Yisra'EL's military failure — it is YHWH withdrawing his presence because the camp is defiled. Verse 19 — my son, give glory to YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL. Yehoshua addresses Akhan with tenderness even while leading him to judgment.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 8 The Taking of Ai — The Covenant Renewed at Mount Eval — The Torah Written on Stones

1 And YHWH said to Yehoshua, Do not fear and do not be dismayed. Take all the fighting men with you, and arise, go up to Ai. See, I have given into your hand the king of Ai, and his people, his city, and his land.

2 And you shall do to Ai and its king as you did to Yerikho and its king. Only its spoil and its livestock you shall take as plunder for yourselves. Lay an ambush against the city, behind it.

3 So Yehoshua and all the fighting men arose to go up to Ai. And Yehoshua chose 30,000 mighty men of valor and sent them out by night.

4 And he commanded them, Behold, you shall lie in ambush against the city, behind it. Do not go very far from the city, but all of you remain ready.

5 And I and all the people who are with me will approach the city. And when they come out against us just as before, we shall flee before them.

6 And they will come out after us, until we have drawn them away from the city. For they will say, They are fleeing from us, just as before. So we will flee before them.

7 Then you shall rise up from the ambush and seize the city, for YHWH your Creator will give it into your hand.

8 And as soon as you have taken the city, you shall set the city on fire. You shall do according to the word of YHWH. See, I have commanded you.

9 So Yehoshua sent them out. And they went to the place of ambush and lay between Beit-El and Ai, to the west of Ai, but Yehoshua spent that night among the people.

10 Yehoshua arose early in the morning and mustered the people and went up, he and the elders of Yisra'EL, before the people to Ai.

11 And all the fighting men who were with him went up and drew near before the city and encamped on the north side of Ai, with a ravine between them and Ai.

12 He took about 5,000 men and set them in ambush between Beit-El and Ai, to the west of the city.

13 So they stationed the forces, the main encampment that was north of the city and its rear guard west of the city. But Yehoshua spent that night in the valley.

14 And as soon as the king of Ai saw this, he and all his people, the men of the city, hurried and went out early to the appointed place toward the Aravah to meet Yisra'EL in battle. But he did not know that there was an ambush against him behind the city.

15 And Yehoshua and all Yisra'EL pretended to be beaten before them and fled in the direction of the wilderness.

16 So all the people who were in the city were called together to pursue them, and as they pursued Yehoshua they were drawn away from the city.

17 Not a man was left in Ai or Beit-El who did not go out after Yisra'EL. They left the city open and pursued Yisra'EL.

18 Then YHWH said to Yehoshua, Stretch out the javelin that is in your hand toward Ai, for I will give it into your hand. And Yehoshua stretched out the javelin that was in his hand toward the city.

19 And the men in the ambush rose quickly out of their place, and as soon as he had stretched out his hand, they ran and entered the city and captured it. And they hurried to set the city on fire.

20 So when the men of Ai looked back, behold, the smoke of the city went up to heaven, and they had no power to flee this way or that, for the people who fled to the wilderness turned back against the pursuers.

21 And when Yehoshua and all Yisra'EL saw that the men in the ambush had captured the city, and that the smoke of the city went up, then they turned back and struck down the men of Ai.

22 And the others came out from the city against them, so they were in the midst of Yisra'EL, some on this side, and some on that side. And Yisra'EL struck them down, until there was left none that survived or escaped.

23 But the king of Ai they took alive, and brought him near to Yehoshua.

24 When Yisra'EL had finished killing all the inhabitants of Ai in the open wilderness where they pursued them, and all of them to the very last had fallen by the edge of the sword, all Yisra'EL returned to Ai and struck it down with the edge of the sword.

25 And all who fell that day, both men and women, were 12,000, all the people of Ai.

26 But Yehoshua did not draw back his hand with which he stretched out the javelin until he had devoted all the inhabitants of Ai to destruction.

27 Only the livestock and the spoil of that city Yisra'EL took as their plunder, according to the word of YHWH that he commanded Yehoshua.

28 So Yehoshua burned Ai and made it forever a heap of ruins, as it is to this day.

29 And he hanged the king of Ai on a tree until evening. And at sunset Yehoshua commanded, and they took his body down from the tree and threw it at the entrance of the gate of the city and raised over it a great heap of stones, which stands there to this day.

30 At that time Yehoshua built an altar to YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, on Mount Eval,

31 Just as Moshe the servant of YHWH had commanded the people of Yisra'EL, as it is written in the Book of the Torah of Moshe, an altar of uncut stones, upon which no man has wielded an iron tool. And they offered on it burnt offerings to YHWH and sacrificed peace offerings.

32 And there, in the presence of the people of Yisra'EL, he wrote on the stones a copy of the Torah of Moshe, which he had written.

33 And all Yisra'EL, sojourner as well as native born, with their elders and officers and their judges, stood on opposite sides of the Ark before the Levitical priests who carried the Ark of the Covenant of YHWH, half of them in front of Mount Gerizim and half of them in front of Mount Eval, just as Moshe the servant of YHWH had commanded at the first, to bless the people of Yisra'EL.

34 And afterward he read all the words of the Torah, the blessing and the curse, according to all that is written in the Book of the Torah.

35 There was not a word of all that Moshe commanded that Yehoshua did not read before all the assembly of Yisra'EL, and the women, and the little ones, and the sojourners who lived among them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves portions of Chapter 8. The DSS version places the covenant renewal ceremony of verses 30-35 earlier in the narrative — after the Yarden crossing and before the battles — rather than after the defeat of Ai as in the Masoretic text. This is a notable DSS variant from the standard Masoretic chapter order.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full. The writing of the Torah on stones at Mount Eval and the public reading to all Yisra'EL — including women, children, and sojourners — is read as the covenant community's foundational act of public Torah transmission upon entering the land.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] Significant variant: 4QJosha (4Q47) places the covenant renewal ceremony of verses 30-35 at a different point in the narrative — after the Yarden crossing and before the battle of Ai. This compilation follows the Masoretic chapter order but notes that the DSS reading presents the covenant ceremony as the first act upon entering the land, before the military campaigns.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 32-35 — the Torah written on stones at Mount Eval and read publicly to all Yisra'EL including women, children, and sojourners. This is the fulfillment of Devarim 27:2-8. The conquest does not begin with fortification or political organization — it begins with the public inscription and proclamation of the Torah. Verse 35 — there was not a word of all that Moshe commanded that Yehoshua did not read. Zero omission. The entire Torah spoken publicly before the whole assembly. The land is entered under the covenant word.

END OF PART ONE — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 8
